message:
perf(core,pager,vdbe,vfs): shared registries, single-connection cache fast path, and retained-commit overlay refinement

Three interconnected performance improvements that reduce per-connection
setup cost and improve :memory: database throughput:

1. Shared builtin registries (connection.rs):
   - Move FunctionRegistry and vtab module registry initialization into
     process-global OnceLock singletons so every new connection clones a
     pre-populated Arc rather than re-registering ~30 functions and ~8
     vtab modules from scratch
   - Only the connection-local ICU collation loader is registered
     per-connection since it captures &Arc<Mutex<CollationRegistry>>

2. Single-connection cache fast path (pager.rs, page_cache.rs):
   - Add enable_single_connection_cache_fast_path() on SimplePager that
     enables an optimized code path in the page cache when the pager is
     still uniquely owned (Arc::get_mut succeeds)
   - Automatically enabled for :memory: databases at construction time
   - Avoids contention overhead on cache operations that can never have
     concurrent readers in the single-connection case

3. Retained memory overlay dirty page tracking (pager.rs, engine.rs):
   - Replace the boolean retained_committed_pages_unflushed flag with a
     BTreeSet<PageNumber> (retained_memory_overlay_dirty_pages) that
     tracks exactly which pages were deferred during :memory: retained
     autocommit
   - New note_retained_memory_overlay_from_write_set() records dirty
     pages without draining the write set
   - materialize_retained_memory_overlay_into_write_set() lazily pulls
     only the pages not already present in the current write set
   - This avoids redundant page copies and enables precise flush at
     release boundaries

Also:
- VDBE engine: extract ConcurrentContext::new() constructor, make
  SharedTxnPageIo.concurrent field Rc<RefCell<>> for interior mutability
  across shared cursors, add MemRowValues type alias with generic
  insert methods
- VFS memory module: add smallvec dependency (Cargo.toml)
- Expand fast_path_separation test coverage for the new overlay behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -8,6 +8,18 @@ This crate implements the FTS5 full-text search extension, the successor to FTS3
 
 This crate sits at the extension layer of the fsqlite workspace. It depends on `fsqlite-types`, `fsqlite-error`, `fsqlite-func` (for `ScalarFunction` and `VirtualTable` traits), and `tracing`.
 
+## Shadow-Backed FTS5 Work
+
+The current crate still reflects FrankenSQLite's existing in-memory/materialized FTS5 path. The first-class shadow-backed backend work is tracked by the FTS5 epic and starts from the implementation contract in [`SHADOW_BACKED_FTS5_CONTRACT_MATRIX.md`](./SHADOW_BACKED_FTS5_CONTRACT_MATRIX.md).
+
+That matrix is the authoritative spec for:
+
+- stock SQLite `rootpage=0` catalog behavior,
+- shadow-table ownership and defensive restrictions,
+- `%_config` / `%_content` / `%_docsize` / `%_data` / `%_idx` responsibilities,
+- content modes, tokenizer/locale/tokendata semantics, and
+- maintenance/integrity command behavior.
+
 ## Key Types
 
 - `Fts5Config` - Configuration for an FTS5 virtual table: content mode, secure-delete, and contentless-delete settings
